description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ruby:2.4.1 Docker image with a postgres:latest service and defines POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_USER and POSTGRES_PASSWORD variables. Include a before_script that runs apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq postgresql postgresql-contrib libpq-dev cmake nodejs libxslt-dev libxml2-dev, then ruby -v, which ruby, gem install bundler --no-ri --no-rdoc, RAILS_ENV=test bundle install --jobs $(nproc) "${FLAGS[@]}", cp config/database.yml.gitlab config/database.yml, and RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rake db:create db:schema:load. Add an rspec job in the test stage that executes RAILS_ENV=test bundle exec rspec. Add a production job of type deploy that runs only on the master branch, installs the dpl gem with gem install dpl, and runs dpl --provider=heroku --app=infocity-poo --api-key=$HEROKU_PRODUCTION_API_KEY to deploy to Heroku. No caches or artifacts are defined.
